title: Revenues and welfare benefits specialist
change: Occupational standard and assessment plan revised
overviewOfRole: Determining liability for local taxation, calculating entitlement to welfare support and advising customers of their rights and responsibilities.

knowledges.detail: Principles of local taxation, including liability, discounts or reliefs, and exemptions.
Types of available benefits, credits and reductions.
Types of customers and their needs.
Indications of customer vulnerability and methods to support vulnerable customers.
Communication tools and methods, including digital, verbal and written.
Techniques to manage challenging or sensitive communications for example active listening, empathy, and de-escalation.
Support available for budgeting, debt or other welfare issues.
Legislation, policies and procedures on rights of dispute or appeal against decisions.
Sources of support, feedback or guidance available to individuals within the organisation.
Methods of obtaining, analysing and validating information and evidence required to support decision making.
Methods to use, record and store personal data securely and in line with relevant data protection requirements.
Key internal and external stakeholders and methods of building and maintaining relationships.
Key performance indicators and own responsibility for contributing to them.
Organisational procedures for service improvement.
Professional development available to align with agreed objectives.
Benefits and value of a diverse and inclusive environment and how legislation and organisational policies support this.
Health and safety legislation and policies relevant to role.
Approaches to sustainability and the efficient use of resources.
Digital applications, tools and systems relevant to role, including the safe use of technology.
The capabilities and benefits, limitations and risks of using AI and digital tools within the role, including organisational policy for appropriate use.
Requirements relating to valuation lists, including relevant legislation, policies, and procedures.
Principles governing liability for local taxation, for example the hierarchy of liability, and associated legislation, policies, and procedures.
Local taxation exemptions, discounts, and reliefs, including applicable legislation, policies, and procedures.
Regulations on additional local taxation charges, including property premiums and court costs, and relevant policies and procedures.
Methods and frequency of local taxation payments, including relevant legislation and procedural guidance.
Processes for local taxation billing, reminders, summons, recovery, and enforcement, including associated legislation and policies.
Eligibility conditions for benefits or reductions, including exclusions from entitlement and claim requirements, as set out in legislation, policies and procedures.
Methods for calculating and commencing benefits or reductions in line with applicable legislation, policies and procedures.
Rules for assessing income and capital within benefit or reduction calculations, as specified in legislation, policies and procedures.
Impact of other household members on the calculation of benefits or reductions, in accordance with legislation, policies and procedures.
Treatment of rent or council tax within benefit or reduction calculations, based on relevant legislation, policies and procedures.
Principles and rules governing effective dates for changes in circumstances and how these affect benefit or reduction calculations, as detailed in legislation, policies and procedures.
Requirements for ensuring entitlement is paid to the correct person using the appropriate method and frequency, in line with legislation, policies and procedures.

Principles for managing overpayments, including determining recoverability, choosing appropriate recovery methods and identifying the correct liable party, as governed by legislation, policies and procedures.

behaviours.detail: Take responsibility for actions, including acknowledging mistakes and taking steps to correct them.
Act with honesty, integrity, professionalism, and impartiality, treating colleagues, service users, and stakeholders fairly and with respect.
Work collaboratively with others, sharing knowledge and contributing positively to team outcomes.
Deliver work efficiently and at pace, managing time effectively to meet expectations while maintaining accuracy and reliability.
Demonstrate dependability, meeting commitments such as attendance, punctuality, and completing assigned tasks.
Show flexibility and resilience, remaining calm and patient in challenging situations and adapting positively to change.
Support an inclusive and respectful environment, valuing different perspectives and encouraging understanding among colleagues and stakeholders.
Apply professional scepticism, recognising potential misuse of systems and verifying information appropriately.

skills.detail: Provide information to customers using a range of communication tools and methods.
Navigate and manage challenging or sensitive communications.
Assist customers with income maximisation, welfare support or budgeting, by signposting or referral.
Notify customers of how they can dispute or appeal decisions as appropriate.
Proactively seek support, feedback, or guidance from colleagues or supervisors where appropriate.
Determine missing, inaccurate or incomplete information and evidence.
Obtain, analyse and validate information and evidence from appropriate sources to support decision making.
Utilise relevant information systems accurately and appropriately, taking account of confidentiality, reputational risks and data security.
Manage internal or external stakeholder relationships
Contribute to the achievement of key performance indicators.
Contribute to service improvement.
Proactively identify and undertake professional development that aligns with agreed objectives.
Comply with equality, diversity and inclusion rules and procedures.
Comply with health and safety requirements in work environment.
Select and appropriately use organisationally relevant digital applications, tools or systems to complete tasks.
Support sustainable methods of working and consider the environmental impact of actions.
Determine local taxation liabilities.
Determine and apply local taxation discounts, exemptions or reliefs.
Establish local taxation additional charges or costs, either applying or withdrawing them.
Arrange local taxation payment methods and frequencies.
Negotiate payment arrangements.
Take actions to recover or enforce debt.
Establish whether customers are eligible for a benefit or reduction and inform them of the decision.
Establish the commencement date for a benefit or reduction.
Assess the impact of income and capital on the calculation of benefits or reductions.
Evaluate the impact of household members on the calculation of benefits or reductions.
Determine how rent or Council Tax affect the calculation of benefits or reductions.
Assess the impact of changes in circumstances on benefit entitlements or reductions.
Determine correct payment or transfer of entitlement to benefits or reductions.
Take action on overpaid benefits or reductions.

eQAProvider.contactAddress:
Ofqual
Earlsdon Park, 53-55 Butts Road, Coventry, CV1 3BH

involvedEmployers: Bolton Council, Cambridge City Council, City of Doncaster Council, Dorset Council, Greater Manchester Combined Authority, Institute of Revenues, Rating and Valuation, Isle of Wight Council, Leicester City Council, Mighty Oak Training Ltd, Milton Keynes City Council, Oldham Council, Peterborough Council, Reigate-Banstead Council, Salford City Council, Salford Council, South West Councils, Tameside Metropolitan Borough Council, Tandridge District Council, Wakefield Metropolitan Council
englishAndMathsQualifications: <p>English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the <a rel="noopener" href="https://www.gov.uk/guidance/apprenticeship-funding-rules" target="_blank">apprenticeship funding rules</a>.</p>
reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Lead and manage a project (business/process improvement), business support services and/or run a practice using the full range of business systems, procedures and accounting practice. Includes the ability to set-up and develop a new business

duties.dutyDetail: Consider statutory and non-statutory frameworks and legislation and apply these as appropriate for the organisation and sector.
Engage with customers, stakeholders and service users through appropriate communication, for example, face to face, digital channels or phone.
Provide accurate information to address and resolve customer enquiries considering their specific needs in a timely and concise manner.
Access and update relevant information systems accurately and appropriately, taking account of confidentiality, reputational risks and data security.
Support the continuous improvement of service delivery using best practice.
Identify opportunities to assist the customer with income maximisation, welfare support or budgeting, whether personal or business related, signposting where appropriate.
Effectively navigate and manage challenging or sensitive communications using skills such as active listening, empathy, and clear communication.
Proactively seek support, feedback, or guidance from colleagues or supervisors when necessary to deal with customer enquiries.
Take ownership of complex and challenging issues, making timely and well-informed decisions in accordance with statutory and non-statutory frameworks and legislation.
Demonstrate personal and professional growth by proactively identifying and undertaking development opportunities that align with agreed objectives.
Apply professional analysis to identify and evaluate missing, inaccurate or incomplete information.
Obtain evidence from appropriate sources, evaluating its validity and relevance and making decisions based on it.
Build and maintain strong stakeholder relationships, including other departments within the organisation and external contacts where appropriate.
Notify customers of how they can dispute or appeal decisions as appropriate.
Support your team by sharing information and offering support to others.
Establish the correct start and end of liability for taxation in line with legislation.
Establish eligibility for reductions or liability to additional charges in local taxation in line with legislation and policies.
Negotiate and arrange payments and payment plans in line with legislation and procedures.
Take appropriate action in relation to collection, recovery and enforcement in line with legislation, policies and procedures.
Assist customers with identifying and accessing financial support (such as national and local benefits, credits or reductions) and calculate entitlement.
Take appropriate action to ensure changes to entitlement are dealt with in line with legislation and within their area of responsibility.
Ensure entitlement has been applied correctly in line with legislation and procedures and within their area of responsibility.
Check overpayments and explain these to customers in line with legislation, policies and procedures.
